programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Steve strikes up a friendship with an attractive blonde, unaware she is a serial killer with a penchant for policemen. Chat show host Sally Jessy Raphael guest stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An exhausted Jesse faces a professional dilemma and finds himself forced to contradict one of Mark's friends to ensure justice is done. Medical detective drama, starring Charlie Schlatter and Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team accepts a job repairing a turbine on a wind farm, but things take a critical turn when Paige and Happy are stranded in a faulty gondola that could plummet at any moment

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter and the team defy the orders of a foreign government as they try to retrieve an asteroid, which is thought to containing DNA strands that could prove deadly if exposed

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs fights for his life on board a US Navy hospital ship after being shot. Meanwhile, DiNozzo and Joanna Teague head for Shanghai, where they hope to take down the leaders of the terrorist group known as The Calling. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Mimi Rogers

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs throws himself back into his work following his near-death experience, updating his appearance and tackling a new risky case alongside DEA agent Luis Mitchell, who wants help bringing down a drugs cartel. Concerned about his colleague's apparent enthusiasm to re-enter the field, DiNozzo discovers Mitchell is the son of a former NCIS agent who gave his life trying to save Gibbs' late wife and daughter, and this attachment is causing Gibbs to break his own rule against personal involvement in a case. However, a significant danger is posed by Benson Long, the cartel's head, and Mitchell's top target

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine is found murdered in Quantico, mere hours after calling Gibbs to discuss a possible case. In response, Bishop and McGee go undercover as a married couple to run surveillance on a lieutenant and his wife, who may be connected to the death. Starring Mark Harmon, Emily Wickersham and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A young Navy Seaman is found dead, with his eyes gouged out, after being involved in a car accident. Director Vance makes a rare return to field duty, pairing up with Gibbs to investigate when it becomes clear the killing seems to be connected to the case of Kip Klugman, a former NCIS agent he once locked up for stealing evidence. Starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter and the team defy the orders of a foreign government as they try to retrieve an asteroid, which is thought to containing DNA strands that could prove deadly if exposed

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and the team are perplexed by Hetty's sudden resignation and press Vance and their new team leader Lauren Hunter for more information about what happened to Hetty. Visiting one of her homes for clues, they find mercenaries ransacking the place - and try to track down the man who hired them. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Hetty confronts Alexa Comescu and tells her Callen is dead, but the crime boss knows she is lying. Meanwhile, the agents search for their leader in Romania hoping to confirm she is alive, but when they mount a rescue attempt one of the team is shot. Real-time episode of the crime drama, starring Linda Hunt, Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The CEO of a software company linked to a cyber-attack against the Department of Defence is kidnapped, and Kensi and Deeks go undercover to protect the man's family after fears over their safety. Meanwhile, Callen continues to research his past

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Sam goes undercover as a bomb specialist when he investigates the death of a demolition expert, killed while driving the car of a notorious club owner. However, he faces a race against time to find explosives located across LA. Meanwhile, Callen learns more about his past and Hunter decides to reshuffle her team

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a reporter is murdered as she prepares to break a story about a mysterious Libyan freedom fighter named El-Libi. Meanwhile, more details of Callen's past begin to emerge

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Following an armed robbery on an antiques shop, Jack agrees to go undercover to nail the dealer responsible for selling the goods - a rogue called Roy Summers, who killed a police officer some years ago and walked free from court on a technicality. The sting goes wrong when Jack loses touch with his back-up team and faces a stern test of his expertise in bric-a-brac. Michael N Harbour guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A man is arrested for attempted murder, but claims he only hit out with a hammer to fight off two knife-wielding intruders and protect his daughters. Emma follows a hunch he is telling the truth and sets up a ruse with fellow officers to prove his innocence. While the team celebrate the result, by-the-book Sergeant Callum Stone is clearly not happy about his junior colleague's maverick methods

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: DC Jacob Banks (Patrick Robinson) joins the Sun Hill watch in time to help a hung-over Mickey investigate the murder of a private detective and soon establishes himself as a member of the team. Meanwhile, Callum continues to single Emma out for harsh treatment among all his PCs and Sally catches the backlash of her resentment. Gordon Kennedy and Anna Calder-Marshall guest star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sally works undercover in a City trading house to investigate the disappearance of 21-year-old Geraldine Lawson. She arranges to go drinking with three of Geraldine's colleagues - all suspects who were with her on the night she went missing. Emma joins the operation as a 'friend' for the night out and proves she'll stop at nothing to get the truth out of her chosen target and put Callum in his place

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor is adjusting to life on her own after her father's wedding. She is engaged to Dr Marsham, but still unsure whether this is the right thing to do

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: A brothel fire causes dark secrets to be revealed and the relationships between Eleanor, Joe and Major Quarrie to be finally resolved

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Girl
programme.desc: Feature-length period drama, starring Siobhan Flynn. A dying woman takes her daughter to the home of her former lover, a wealthy mine agent, who takes the child in. But the man's wife is less than happy about the arrangement and determines to keep the girl apart from her own offspring. With Jonathan Cake, Mark Benton and Susan Jameson

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The government plans to close down small hospitals in favour of larger establishments. Dr Turner and Shelagh prepare for an inspection that will decide the fate of Nonnatus House - but the staff is shocked to find Sister Ursula has no intention of helping to save the maternity home. Barbara comes to the aid of a woman from the Chinese community who is struggling to get on with her mother-in-law, and whose baby is seriously ill

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the drama. A couple wake up to find a strangled corpse on the hearthrug in their library - but help is at hand in the diminutive form of venerable amateur sleuth Miss Marple, who just happens to live next door. She agrees to investigate but, as her inquiries get under way, another body is discovered. Joan Hickson, Gwen Watford and Moray Watson star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The body of a missing teenager is found and ambitious DCI Claire Ashby is committed to proving the guilt of her main suspect, a local locksmith

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Matt and Charlie's marriage is put to the test when they disagree over Ivy's future, and Hugh's plan to leave Whyhope is scuppered when he is accused of malpractice

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Ajax takes desperate measures to stop Hugh selling the farm, Penny discovers her perfect match thanks to Meryl's dating service, and Matt gets drunk with April

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey argues with Darryl about the lack of trust in their relationship, and visits a therapist for advice on how to resolve the issue. Comedy, starring Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon is convinced her 30th birthday is just the start of another three decades of misery, prompting Tracey to question the wisdom of throwing a party. Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of a three-part story. Emma and Sally are first to respond when two massive explosions rock Sun Hill. John and Gina monitor the shocking scenes of carnage from the station, while Callum takes charge on the ground, directing his team to deal with multiple casualties, confused and hysterical survivors and petty thieves taking advantage of the chaos. When it becomes clear a pervert has been posing as a doctor to molest injured women, Callum loses his grip and vents his fury on the man, but his troubles have only just begun

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Oliver faces the prospect of repeating his F1 year if he makes another mistake, and when a past error returns to haunt him, he takes desperate action to avoid suffering such a fate. Meanwhile, Jac's ruthlessness makes an unwelcome return when she discovers one of Greg's patients is a candidate for cutting-edge surgery, and Donna wonders whether she is strong enough to handle the casualties of war after visiting an injured Kieran

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ellen begs Abs not to tell anyone the truth after he discovers her secret, while Luke is plagued by a series of hoax calls. Maggie feels upstaged by a rival bidding to win a new consultancy post and a woman who has miscarried continues to let her husband believe she is still pregnant. Former Hollyoaks star Katherine Dow Blyton guests

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. The Sun Hill team struggle to come to terms with Emma's death and Callum's guilty feelings lead to a fight with Smithy, while Sally can only weep after arresting chief suspect Carl Adams. DI Karen Lacy (Liz May Brice) takes over the case for the Counter Terrorism Unit and Kezia is sent with Mickey to search the bomb-maker's home, only to find she has walked into a booby trap

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Lorraine finds Joe's bottles of medication in the bin and asks him why he's thrown them away. Joe's determined that he feels fine and does not want them any more

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sanjay surprises Gita. Sonia's worries unite Carol and Alan. Phil begs Kathy to give him one more chance

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Jessica Bailey finds it a struggle to fit in amongst the regiment, and an officer makes Chris McLeod jealous after he returns from leave

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried has to deal with an outbreak of foot-and-mouth disease, while the lack of gas masks for pets causes concern. Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Jane helps the roguish antiques dealer search for a valuable Yeats painting in Ireland, until a more important matter captures their attention. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: The couple try to get slim _ but Hester's diet plans do not appeal to William's taste, especially when alcohol is also restricted. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana investigate a spate of mysterious thefts at Bayview, and Tom ends up facing a shoplifting charge - but Diana decides to defend him

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy makes a shocking discovery when he learns he may be a member of the aristocracy. Meanwhile, Compo concludes that wearing a hat is his ticket to winning Nora Batty's heart. Brian Wilde and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo decides to build a home-made trampoline following a trip to the seaside. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, starring Bill Owen

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: First episode of the crime drama following high-flying solicitor Jane Kennedy as she takes over the job of coroner in the English coastal town she escaped from as a teenager. A teenager is found dead at the foot of a tower and DS Davey Higgins believes it was a suicide, but Jane thinks there is more to the case than meets the eye. Starring Claire Goose and Matt Bardock

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: The maverick detective returns to work after a traumatic arrest to investigate the murder of a former child genius's parents, and quickly deduces that their daughter is responsible. However, unable to find evidence to convict, he becomes embroiled in a battle of wits with the suspect - who has set her sights on him and his estranged wife. Idris Elba stars in the first instalment of the acclaimed detective drama, with Indira Varma, Steven Mackintosh, Saskia Reeves, Paul McGann and Ruth Wilson

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The penultimate episode sees the retired detectives investigating possibly their most difficult cold case yet. A bloodstained bust is uncovered during construction work in a cemetery and the evidence points to the murder of an alternative-medicine practitioner seven years earlier. Further clues lead to the controversial world of cryopreservation - the practice of keeping clinically dead patients frozen to extend their life cycle. Sasha is determined they don't put a foot wrong - but with the sobering possibility that two perpetrators are still at large, the detectives are forced to go out on a limb

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The Lyell team investigates the murder of a family shot while picnicking by a river, and discover the female victim was in the middle of a bitter divorce. Local detective Malcolm Guillam is brought in to help and when the present-day murder reminds him of an unsolved case that haunts him, he sees an opportunity to redeem himself. However, DCI Sally Butcher is convinced that the woman's estranged husband is responsible for the murder and has no time for Guillam's trip down memory lane. Feature-length episode, guest starring Derek Griffiths

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The monks cause a storm of resentment by launching an expedition to a Welsh village to recover the bones of St Winifred and return them to the abbey. As if that does not make them unpopular enough, a local landowner dies in mysterious circumstances and it is up to Brother Cadfael to clear his colleagues of murder and ensure they get out of the country alive. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Michael Culver, John Hallam and Anna Friel

programme.desc: Part three of three. Callum vows to trace the bomber responsible for his colleague's death and another alert gives the plucky sergeant a chance to demonstrate his bravery once more. CID are horrified to realise they have had the killer in the station all along and let him get away. The Sun Hill watch pay their respects at Emma's graveside. Liz May Brice and Mark Moraghan guest star

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy makes Phil face up to his alcoholism. Ricky is shocked to discover what's been going on at The Arches. Joe worries Lorraine. Sanjay tries to avoid the doctor's test results

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil has to try harder if he wants to see Ben. Bianca goes to Spain. Tony's looking for a job. Lorraine and Joe don't go to Fiji - Joe hasn't taken his medication

programme.desc: The CO has a lot on his mind after a civilian gets caught in the crossfire during a training exercise, causing an embittered Mark to fly off the handle. Julie has a surprise for Tony and Jacko, but ends up learning a harsh lesson, while Karen's son Kevin wonders if he might be gay

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James visits the dog track dash, but the greyhound owners consider him a touch too conscientious when his services are called upon. Tristan stirs up trouble when he tries his hand at cooking. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Charlie Gimbert plots revenge, while Lovejoy and Lady Jane bicker over a royal dinner service belonging to a Belgian countess. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester makes plans to stand for election to the local residents' association and enlists William to help with her speech. However, she soon finds her success jeopardised by an attractive rival. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom opts for a policy of complete honesty at all times, while Diana tries to cope with her niece, the 'Clapham Strangler'. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard has had enough of Pearl and decides to leave home for ever. However, he soon finds he misses her, so the trio try to reunite the couple. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brian Wilde, with Robert Fyfe

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy decides to go trekking across the moors, but his adventure coincides with Auntie Wainwright's special offer of grocery delivery bicycles. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Havers returns to work and is forced to confront demons from her past as the team investigates the case of a woman murdered with a shotgun. A cryptic word painted at the crime scene and the discovery of a mysterious photo lead the police to think the killer is sending them clues, and further investigations uncover a secret the local community has been trying to hide

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a political activist 15 years earlier but, just as it is getting somewhere, Ted orders the investigation to be halted and Ucos is threatened with closure following its perceived mishandling of the Hanway case. However, the team refuses to give up, and finds evidence of a cover-up involving top officials and a multi-national drugs company. Guest starring Meera Syal and Lorraine Ashbourne

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A severed finger is brought to the Lyell Centre by DCI Rory Goodchild and immigration enforcement officer Pia Butler, who ask Nikki and Thomas to test for a DNA match with 17 year-old Syrian illegal immigrant Akka Khoury. The teenager was sent the digit by a people-smuggler who claims to have her mother and is demanding money for her safe return. Meanwhile, Akka's 12 year-old cousin Sosa is trying to enter the UK illegally hidden in a van on a cross-channel ferry. Emilia Fox stars

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The annual St Peter's Fair gets under way in Shrewsbury but a gruesome murder dampens the festivities. Antagonism between local merchants and visiting traders becomes increasingly ugly, and as civil unrest rages in the streets, Beringar is ordered to kill his friend Cadfael, whose investigations are causing King Stephen acute political embarrassment. Medieval whodunit, starring Derek Jacobi, Eoin McCarthy and Michael Culver

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Newcastle United v West Ham United at St James' Park and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Sheffield United at Molineux. The Magpies and Hammers are in need of points for differing reasons, with Newcastle battling to avoid relegation and West Ham hoping to qualify for Europe. Meanwhile, Wolves will hope to avoid become embroiled in their own fight for survival and took on a team that looks almost certain to go down

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final, which came from Wembley Stadium

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her favourite tales of faith, hope and spirituality, when ordinary people do extraordinary things

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore an impressive new build in Cottishall, Norfolk, a terraced house in Bootle in Liverpool and a house with an outside toilet in Maidstone, Kent

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Gary Pe help two teams search for antiques in Stamford Meadows, Lincolnshire. Host Natasha Raskin Sharp views a special ceiling painting at Grimsthorpe Castle

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins talks to The Archbishop of York, Stephen Cottrell, about the role of Christianity after lockdown, and comedian Frank Skinner reflects on his relationship with God

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton guides a pair of budding entrepreneurs who want to escape to the Cotswolds. They have a generous £900,000 to play with, but the property must have the potential to develop a boutique country holiday let. Alistair also spends an exhilarating day at the University of Bath as he launches himself on to the UK's only bobsleigh and skeleton push-start track

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team reclaim items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with Jay Blades attempting to rejuvenate a set of classic dining chairs

programme.title: The Good Dinosaur (2015)
programme.desc: In a world where the asteroid impact that wiped out the dinosaurs never happened, humans have evolved as a primitive species while the giant lizards remain the dominant life form on Earth. A young apatosaurus becomes separated from his family, but finds a friend in a feral human boy as he makes the dangerous journey home. Pixar animated adventure, with the voices of Raymond Ochoa and Jack Bright

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher goes wild in the Hambleden Valley in Buckinghamshire, on a whistle-stop tour of a chocolate-box landscape that has been the backdrop for many a TV drama and film. He also finds out how the old cottage industry of lace-making is poised for a comeback, and saddles up to meet a farming couple opening up their farm to enable safer riding in this corner of the countryside. Adam Henson is busy with some new arrivals and, as picking season dawns, Charlotte Smith investigates whether UK farmers can cover a shortfall in the number of seasonal farm workers needed to gather their harvests

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Newby Hall, near Ripon in North Yorkshire, Treasures include a letter sent by Roald Dahl to a primary school, a Chippendale chair worth a king's ransom and football memorabilia harking back to the early days of the beautiful game - when a week's wages at Arsenal were less than the price of a cup of coffee today

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Controversial plans are afoot to introduce a private clinic to Nonnatus House. The proposition leads to the first disagreement in 20 years between Sister Julienne, who sees the potential to generate income, and Dr Turner, who opposes private healthcare on principle. Shelagh feels caught in the middle of their conflict, while Trixie is delighted by the opportunity of working at the new clinic. Sister Monica Joan's crisis of faith continues to trouble her, a baby is born with no legs below the knees, and Lucille helps Cyril find the perfect accommodation that will benefit him and the Church

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: AC-12 link Gail Vella's murder with a historic case of police corruption, and Kate hatches a plan to tell once and for all if Jo is bent. They are closer than ever to cracking the case, but when Hastings' authority is undermined, it leaves his team in a potentially dangerous situation. Starring Vicky McClure, Kelly Macdonald and Adrian Dunbar

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from Arsenal v Fulham at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v Burnley at Old Trafford in the Premier League, as the Gunners and United both looked to complete doubles over their opponents following away wins earlier in the campaign

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Leicester City v Southampton. Action from the concluding semi-final, which came from Wembley Stadium

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love and Jamali Maddix are joined by reality star Spencer Matthews and model Vogue Williams, aiming to identify the real Mitch from a line-up of four. Mitch is a interiors enthusiast, who has lovingly created a green-heavy home in Hinckley near Leicester. Hosted by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts explores a flat in Streatham, London, and a plot of land in the South Wales village of Tonyrefail, and Dion Dublin visits a cottage in Saddleworth, Lancashire

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A woman's personal injury claim bites back after claiming that metal shards were found in her takeaway and a bishop faces judgement after trying to pull off a £12,000 car fraud

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: An Aberdeen crew on nightshift searches for a woman at risk of hypothermia, while the air ambulance transfers a patient from his home on Lewis to Glasgow for hospital treatment

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Peterborough, and uncovers the history of pub interiors. Charles Hanson and David Harper are on hand to advise the red and blue teams

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie sees omens everywhere, and Bear can't see what's in front of him. Zara has to step in when a one-night stand has disastrous consequences

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a Kent-based couple find a property in Herefordshire on a £700,000 budget. Away from the houses, Jules meets two farmers who turn their potato crop into crisps

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore calls on designer Rupert Blanchard and upcycler Sarah Peterson to rescue a once-cherished sewing box, some sash windows and a dolls house

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a 1930s pinball machine, an antique weather device and a rocking horse that holds huge sentimental significance for its owner

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Labour candidate for the London mayoral election.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Clive Myrie investigates allegations of racism in the Church of England. A year after the archbishop of Canterbury, Justin Welby, admitted that the Church was still 'deeply institutionally racist', Clive meets vicars, curates and theological trainees to understand the scale of the problem. He hears stories of racist abuse and claims of a culture that creates a hostile environment for Christians of colour, who have suffered in silence for fear of further discrimination or losing their jobs

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush and Whitney contemplate the future, setting in motion an exciting plan for a new adventure away from Walford. Mick hopes a few family board games will encourage Nancy and Frankie to bond, and Tiffany and Dotty are forced to come clean to Sonia about overcharging the punters at work

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Basil decides to enhance the hotel's reputation with an exclusive night of gourmet cuisine. His new chef has a cordon bleu rating and loves every type of dish - but he is particularly taken with Manuel. Classic comedy, written by and starring John Cleese and Connie Booth. First broadcast in 1975

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Greta sets off to the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, to call for an immediate reduction in fossil fuel use, but is frustrated by what she sees as a media obsession constantly pitting her against Donald Trump. As she travels across Europe, Greta meets Polish miners who fear that climate change policies will further decimate their industry and culture, and discovers the prototype of a technology that aims to capture carbon dioxide from factory emissions before it enters the atmosphere. She also meets one of her inspirations - Sir David Attenborough

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: Faryal has big plans for her makeup launch. Amir tries to sign his first pro-boxing client. Zaviyar's 1st birthday is a bittersweet affair, as the family receives devastating news

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: The Khans buy a holiday home in Dubai, where big news breaks, Faryal freaks about not having a dress for the awards and Amir hangs out with some soccer stars

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Tom Cruise, Jennifer Connelly and Miles Teller join Graham to talk about the much-anticipated release of Top Gun: Maverick. Felicity Jones discusses her romantic drama The Last Letter from Your Lover, Wunmi Mosaku chats about Bafta-nominated His House, and John Bishop promotes his tour Right Here, Right Now. Plus, music from Years & Years - fronted by It's a Sin's Olly Alexander

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic legend Sarah Storey, former England cricketer Ryan Sidebottom, world champion swimmer Mark Foster and European gold medal-winning sprinter Adam Gemili join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A static caravan is bought for the plot of land it stands on in Dunkirk, Kent. Meanwhile, a semi in Olton in the Midlands and a terrace in South Norwood in London are also sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Lucy Alexander meet the new owners to hear plans for their buys

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Questions are asked when a year after a house fire a second claim comes in, while the data embedded in digital photographs submitted to support stolen items does not add up

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, a call about a possible person in the Clyde triggers a huge emergency response, while in Edinburgh, the Special Operations team are called to a fire in a block of flats

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Jonathan Pratt give advice to two teams at Wetherby racecourse, with Charlie Ross overseeing proceedings also showing off his collection of gavels

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Flirting is in the air - and while Daniel is flattered, Bear is horrified. A dog's death leads Rob to uncover a dark secret

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple find a new home in Somerset where they can create their very own back garden vegetable patch. Away from the search, Sonali also learns about the potentially perilous Bristol Channel and meets the volunteers who operate two life-saving rescue hovercrafts

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues faded curtains, a 1950s chest of drawers and an old storage larder from the tip, enlisting bag-maker Rosanna Gethin and screen-print artist Zoe Murphy to help transform them

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts use their restoration skills on an ancient penny farthing bicycle, a battered leather briefcase and a clocking-on clock from the British Rail depot in Crewe

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Conservative candidate for the London mayoral election.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A frustrated Nancy tries to track down a lost package, leaving her family curious over what could be so important - especially when she refuses to look after Ollie. Isaac has questions about his family, and Bernie is surprised when Molly apologises and asks her out for a coffee

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Louis feels trapped as Vicky's blackmailing escalates, and he wonders how much longer he can keep his double life a secret. Max grows more determined to catch the hospital thief, a desperate Dom campaigns for Ange to assist with his operation and Kian leads Lucky on a merry dance across the hospital as he tries to dodge a drugs test

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Patients from Britain's most remote community - on Fair Isle, south of Shetland - wait for a weather window to make the trip to Lerwick for important operations

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The friends desperately rush Roxy to hospital but are horrified to discover that emergency medical treatment is hugely expensive. Unless they can get the money to pay it off, Roxy won't be allowed to leave the country. In all the confusion, Keeley realises they have missed out on their kidnap plan. Time is running out for the syndicate - and they are still no closer to getting proof. Unknown to them, however, Frank is confronted by Mr Shriver, the head of Mercury Millions' fraud team. Does he know about the stolen ticket?

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The contest for aspiring make-up artists returns, with new host Maya Jama. The contestants' first professional assignment sees them creating an eye-catching look for a Superdrug beauty campaign. In the creative brief, judges Val Garland and Dominic Skinner ask the artists to show what makes them unique. Only then do the judges decide which two will take on the first elimination face-off

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman questions Norma's faith in him, and Romero's presented with evidence that may change the course of Miss Watson's murder investigation. Meanwhile, Zane's thirst for revenge threatens Dylan. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, based on Robert Bloch's novel of the same name, depicting the life of Norman Bates and his mother Norma prior to the events portrayed in the film

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma refuses to tell Norman the truth about his blackouts, while Romero digs deeper into the death of Miss Watson. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, based on Robert Bloch's novel of the same name, depicting the life of Norman Bates and his mother Norma prior to the events portrayed in the film

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets members of the community of Breaston in Derbyshire, as they aim to create a centrepiece Bug Hotel in their first entry into the Britain In Bloom competition

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In the cottage garden at Longmeadow, Monty Don offers advice on what to plant under shrubs and roses. On a visit to Dunsborough Park in Surrey, Carol Klein celebrates a spectacular display of tulips, and Kate Bradbury goes through the first stages of creating a wildlife garden in Brighton

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Tom Heap and Margherita Taylor look at the impact of canals on the history of the British countryside. They the Grand Union Canal and celebrating the 200th anniversary of the Regent's Canal, a once-commercial route that now brings a slice of countryside to the city, benefitting both residents and wildlife. Plus, wildlife cameraman Jack Perks documents some frogs, and an update on the fortunes of a family staking their future on the success of high-tech farming

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits Tentsmuir National Nature Reserve in Fife, to learn how the reserve has coped with the dramatic surge in visitors during lockdown, as local people reconnected with the nature on their doorstep. He also assesses how the farming industry is coping post-Brexit. Euan Mcilwraith looks back on the lessons learned as we mark the 20th anniversary of the foot-and-mouth outbreak, while in Sutherland Anne Lundon finds out what it was like to attend the remotest school in Britain. Arlene Stuart reports from the banks of the Forth, hoping to catch sight of a humpback whale called Barney

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Six budding gardeners and families will make regular appearances throughout the series to share hints from their own gardens all over Scotland to encourage us all to get growing. Mairi and Carole give Beechgrove a good old spring clean. While George is down at his allotment, Kirsty takes a day out at the beach to go searching for seaweed

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Wales must go head to head and cook their six-course menus again, but will they take on board their veteran's advice? They serve a panel of judges and guest judge Hugh Hunt from the world of maths and engineering, whose skills are used to work out how to do daredevil stunts on TV

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day two at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding session of Yan Bingtao's first-round contest against Martin Gould and the opening frames of Anthony McGill v Ricky Walden

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The return of the sewing competition in which 12 keen amateurs take part in three challenges, all judged by Savile Row tailor Patrick Grant and fashion designer Esme Young. The sewers start in their efforts to impress with the first pattern challenge, creating the simple, clean lines of the versatile classic sleeveless blouse called a 'shell top', and, for the transformation challenge, they must turn old T-shirts into completely new garments for men, women or children, in just 90 minutes. In the made-to-measure, mannequins are replaced with real-life models, but who will whip up a well-fitting buffet dress and win themselves garment of the week, and whose hopes of staying in the competition will flounder? Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day two at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where David Gilbert concludes his first-round contest against Chris Wakelin and four-time champion John Higgins gets his campaign under way against Tian Pengfei

programme.title: Down the Mighty River with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist travels down the Baliem River in New Guinea from its source to the sea, hoping to encounter the ancient tribes and dangerous wildlife that live long its banks and in the surrounding jungle. He begins his journey inland at Lake Habbema, and braves some of the world's fiercest white water rapids. Along the way, he stays in a village belonging to the Dani tribe and ventures into some previously unexplored caves

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary following life on farms around Scotland, from Argyll to the Outer Hebrides. This time, a new family of dairy farmers, the McLeans, are introduced. Meanwhile, Raymond goes the extra mile to save his lambs, Stevie faces buffalo cow number 666, and Kirsty and Aimee take last year's calves to market

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan visits Bangladesh's Ganges delta, which is under threat from increasingly extreme weather, and joins a government effort to plant huge new areas with mangroves to defend the low-lying country from tidal surges. He then travels to the kingdom of Bhutan to discover how melting Himalayan glaciers affect the entire world, but also hears from the prime minister how his country has become carbon negative

programme.title: Australia: Earth's Magical Kingdom
programme.desc: How the country's surrounding oceans - the Southern Sea, the Pacific and the Indian Ocean - create a unique environment for numerous creatures. In the waters off south Australia, sealions, once a rare sight, are now thriving, while in the wide shallows of Spencer Gulf, a mid-winter gathering of colourful giant cuttlefish battle for mates. Off the west coast, in the warm currents of the Indian Ocean, brown boobies build makeshift nests, while further south tiger sharks sweep in to prey on a whale carcass. Narrated by Barry Humphries

programme.title: I, Tonya (2017)
programme.desc: Ice skater Tonya Harding rises through the ranks at the US figure skating championships, but her future in the sport is thrown into question by a shocking incident after her ex-husband steps in. Director Craig Gillespie's comedy biopic, starring Margot Robbie, Sebastian Stan, and Allison Janney, who won a Bafta and an Oscar for her performance

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day two in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of Stephen Maguire v Jamie Jones and Neil Robertson against Liang Wenbo

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: A look back at the first-round matches on the second day of the tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Claire becomes increasingly suspicious of Samuel's motives, and Hiro returns to Japan, where he urges Ando to help him rescue Mohinder from a psychiatric institution. Peter struggles to cope with Nathan's traumatic death, and Noah launches his plan to destroy the carnival, helped by Edgar. Drama, starring Hayden Panettiere

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Sylar seeks out the carnival to obtain a batch of powers, but discovers a new weakness. Meanwhile, Claire and Peter try to resolve their emotions at Nathan's wake, and Noah and Lauren capture Edgar in an effort to determine what he knows. Starring Zachary Quinto

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The outcome of his compensation case against the hospital inspires Dominic to refashion his life, but when his plans are dashed, he's soon asking a huge favour of Sacha. Kian's bromance with a troubled patient takes a surprising turn when he and Chloe make a shocking discovery in theatre, while money matters continue to mount for Max

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs a topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the viirtual audience

programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques Fair. Presenter Charlie Ross gets the lowdown on the resurgence of brown furniture, before the whole team heads to Derby for some auction action

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: Tasty treats are on the menu for the monkeys and chimps, while the fast-disappearing Scottish wildcat is thrown a lifeline and it is time for two zebras to begin a new life in Fife. Meanwhile, there are efforts under way at the Highland Wildlife Park to preserve one of Scotland's most iconic species - the Scottish wildcat

programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day three at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of John Higgins' contest against Tian Pengfei and the start of Jack Lisowski's campaign against Ali Carter

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: Presenter Sabrina Grant brings in a box of her own precious items for a transformation, a collection of denim clothing that belonged to her sister, who died suddenly 10 years ago

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers get back on their bikes to explore the culinary delights of several Mediterranean countries, discovering hearty and healthy food from France, Spain and Italy. They begin by searching for the authentic flavours of Italy, from simple local ingredients to spicy nduja pate and some of the best gelato around

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the fifth semi-final of the quiz, with four more returning contenders answering questions on specialist subjects of Christopher Eccleston, Metallica, the life and works of Beatrix Potter, and the Greco-Persian Wars, before they each are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the sixth and concluding semi-final of the quiz, with four returning contenders answering questions on the specialist subjects of William Pitt the Younger, The Bridge, the Grand National, and the plays of Joe Orton, before they are each tested on general knowledge

programme.title: Different League: The Derry City Story
programme.desc: Documentary about how football club Derry City made a comeback in 1985 after withdrawing from the Irish League in 1972, when they were accepted to play in the League of Ireland. Featuring interviews with Tony O'Doherty, Terry Harkin, Eddie Mahon and Eamonn McLaughlin, who were known as the Gang of Four and were the men behind the club's rebirth, as well as rare archive footage and memories of faithful supporters

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Line of Duty star Adrian Dunbar hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Katherine Ryan and journalist Tim Shipman joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.desc: A look back at the first-round matches on the third day of the tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Tom Heap and Margherita Taylor look at the impact of canals on the history of the British countryside. They the Grand Union Canal and celebrating the 200th anniversary of the Regent's Canal, a once-commercial route that now brings a slice of countryside to the city, benefitting both residents and wildlife. Plus, wildlife cameraman Jack Perks documents some frogs, and an update on the fortunes of a family staking their future on the success of high-tech farming

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at a celebrity's life through 10 defining photos, with the star's secrets revealed by those who were there, and those who knew them best. The first edition focuses on Queen frontman Freddie Mercury, beginning with the earliest photo of the singer as a baby, and ending with a private moment captured shortly before his death

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Culzean Castle in South West Scotland, and uncovers the story of a former slave who established himself at the heart of the local community. Treasures brought in to be valued include a 1914 FA cup medal, a valuable Art Nouveau clock, and a huge statue found in a compost heap

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don adds self-sown ornamental grass seedlings to the paradise garden at Longmeadow, and Advolly Richmond explores the extraordinary history of lawns. Plus, Adam Frost explores the front gardens in a London street

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Wakefield Trinity, Hull FC v Warrington Wolves and Castleford Tigers v Leigh Centurions

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day four at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, including the conclusion of Jack Lisowski's first-round match against Ali Carter and the opening frames of Mark Allen v Lyu Haotian

programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day four at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where Ding Junhui's first-round match against Stuart Bingham concludes and 2019 champion Judd Trump plays the opening frames of his contest against Liam Highfield

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: The transformation of a beloved car leaves its owner astounded, and a pair of ballet shoes are given a new lease of life. Hosted by Sabrina Grant

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of Sardinia, starting out in Cagliari, where everyone is in high spirits for the feast of St Efisio. The bikers join in by cooking a local speciality, almond and orange cakes. They also meet the tuna fishermen of Carloforte and a chef who has a special way of serving up platters of the fish, are shown how to make sheep's black pudding by a group of shepherds and are served a stew by the 'King of Sardinia'

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Taking place in a specially built workshop nestled in Birmingham's historic Jewellery Quarter, Katherine Ryan hosts as talented jewellers battle it out to prove they can be the next big jewellery-making sensation. This time, the seven remaining contestants are challenged to make a chain collar and a sweetheart brooch, before one of them leaves the competition

programme.title: Makeup: A Glamorous History
programme.desc: Makeup artist Lisa Eldridge explores what the beauty looks of three iconic moments in British history reveal about that era, beginning with the high Georgians. Lisa goes to Chatsworth House in Derbyshire in search of one of the beauty icons of the day, Georgiana, Duchess of Devonshire, and discovers how in this period of extreme wealth in Britain, the rich entered an arms race of beauty. Later, she recreates an authentic Georgian look on a 21st century model

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Ardal O'Hanlon plays host in this edition of the comedy showcase, recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. The show features sets by Mawaan Rizwan and Sara Barron

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day four at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, including the concluding session of Mark Allen's first-round match against Lyu Hoatian

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: A look back at the first-round matches on the fourth day of the tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Shooting Joe Exotic
programme.desc: Louis revisits his 2011 documentary America's Most Dangerous Pets, returning to Oklahoma to explore what has happened since. Joe Exotic, aka Joseph Allen Maldonado-Passage, now resides in a federal prison after being found guilty of a murder-for-hire plot and multiple animal cruelty charges, and having been the subject of hit Netflix documentary Tiger King

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness confront the male mid-life crisis by buying the perfect sports cars for a middle-aged gentleman. Back at the track, Chris tests out the raucous new Toyota GR Yaris hot hatch

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace present the first episode of finals week, in which the five remaining contenders must cook a five-course lunch at the City of London's Plaisterers Hall for members of the hospitality sector who delivered thousands of meals to frontline workers during the first lockdown. The contestants must each serve up as dish of their own creation, inspired by their own lockdown experience, then return to the studio to make culinary tributes to their heroes

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day two at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding sessions of Stephen Maguire v Jamie Jones and Neil Robertson v Liang Wenbo. Maguire lost 10-3 to Martin Gould at this stage last year, while Robertson overcame Liang 10-5, but there was to be no repeat of his 2010 title win, as he was beaten 13-7 by Mark Selby in the quarter-finals

programme.title: Call the Midwife: The Casebook
programme.desc: Stephen McGann presents a look at the true stories behind the drama series, meeting midwives, nuns and doctors who worked delivering babies during the 1950s and 1960s to hear their accounts of the social and medical changes of the period. He talks to a former colleague of Jennifer Worth, writer of the original Call the Midwife books, and a photographer who documented the social conditions of the time. Stephen also returns to the home where he was born to share his mother's own story of childbirth

programme.title: Between the Lines
programme.desc: Tony is called in to investigate complaints against an officer who was killed while on duty. Police drama, starring Neil Pearson, Tom Georgeson and Siobhan Redmond

programme.title: Between the Lines
programme.desc: Clark accuses Sgt Markham of corruption, while Jenny finds herself under increasing pressure over her part in the Carswell affair. Starring Neil Pearson, Tom Georgeson and Siobhan Redmond

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores how the public's view of Roman Britain has changed over the decades, examining events from the initial invasion, through to the construction of Hadrian's Wall and the eventual collapse of the empire, drawing on the work of archaeologists and historians and using 50 years of BBC archive footage. With contributions from Charlotte Higgins, Adrian Goldsworthy, Neil Faulkner and Jo Quinn

programme.title: This Green and Pleasant Land: The Story of British Landscape Painting
programme.desc: Artists, critics and academics examine depictions of the British landscape in art. The programme charts the genre's development from the earliest Flemish paintings in the court of Charles I to David Hockney's digital drawings. Featuring contributions by film-maker Nic Roeg, historian Dan Snow and novelist Will Self

programme.title: Call the Midwife: The Casebook
programme.desc: Stephen McGann presents a look at the true stories behind the drama series, meeting midwives, nuns and doctors who worked delivering babies during the 1950s and 1960s to hear their accounts of the social and medical changes of the period. He talks to a former colleague of Jennifer Worth, writer of the original Call the Midwife books, and a photographer who documented the social conditions of the time. Stephen also returns to the home where he was born to share his mother's own story of childbirth

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day three at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding sessions of Anthony McGill v Ricky Walden and Kyren Wilson v Gary Wilson. McGill recorded a 10-9 victory over Jack Lisowski at this stage last year, while Wilson received a bye following the withdrawal of Anthony Hamilton. The two would eventually meet in the semi-finals with Wilson edging an epic encounter 17-16, but having no answer to Ronnie O'Sullivan in the final

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: As the red carpet season reaches its climax, film critic Mark Kermode looks back at past winners of the prestigious award, and celebrates some of the most memorable performances

programme.title: Stephen: The Murder That Changed a Nation
programme.desc: Documentary examining the 1993 death of Stephen Lawrence, beginning with a look at events leading up to his murder and the police investigation that follows. As the suspects remain free, tip-offs from the community make his parents, Doreen and Neville, wonder why the authorities are not making arrests

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Cosmologists discuss their project to create a map of everything in existence, and reveal that their research has yielded some highly unexpected results, creating a picture stranger than anything they had imagined. Scientists also explain why the map suggests the universe may not be an all-encompassing entity - but merely the starting point for something much bigger

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of a widowed 83-year-old who went undercover in a Chilean nursing home to investigate a private detective's concerns that the residents, including her father, were being abused. Despite an initial struggle with the technology involved, the aged spy adapted to his role, only to discover that the truth about the institution was very different to what his client had expected

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: As the red carpet season reaches its climax, film critic Mark Kermode looks back at past winners of the prestigious award, and celebrates some of the most memorable performances

programme.title: Stephen: The Murder That Changed a Nation
programme.desc: Documentary examining the 1993 death of Stephen Lawrence, beginning with a look at events leading up to his murder and the police investigation that follows. As the suspects remain free, tip-offs from the community make his parents, Doreen and Neville, wonder why the authorities are not making arrests

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day four at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding session of Mark Allen's first-round match against Lyu Haotian and the opening frames of Barry Hawkins' contest against Matthew Selt. Allen has participated in this event in each of the last 14 years, but his best showing remains his semi-final appearance in 2009, while 'The Hawk' was runner-up to Ronnie O'Sullivan in 2013

programme.title: A303: Highway to the Sun
programme.desc: Tom Fort travels along the 92-mile-long A303 from the Hampshire town of Basingstoke to Honiton in Devon. Originally designed in the 1960s, the route has been the subject of many proposed upgrades - several of which were strongly opposed due to their potential impact on sites of historic and natural significance, including Stonehenge. The programme charts the highway's turbulent history, as well as examining the development of the region over the past 5,000 years

programme.title: Stephen: The Murder That Changed a Nation
programme.desc: The second police investigation reveals shocking surveillance footage of the gang alleged to have killed Stephen, and with the help of their lawyer Imran Khan, Doreen and Neville Lawrence launch the first private murder prosecution in 150 years. However, when it collapses, questions about the failure of the first police investigation mount

programme.title: Lights Up
programme.desc: Adaptation of the National Theatre of Scotland's award-winning stage play about the life of a young transgender man, inspired by the life of Adam Kashmiry, who also takes the lead role. Born in Egypt, Adam was classed as female at birth but always knew he was male. Trapped in the wrong body, in a deeply conservative society, he knew he had to escape. What followed was beyond Adam's wildest dreams. Written by Frances Poet

programme.title: Colour: The Spectrum of Science
programme.desc: Early Earth was the canvas for a vast new palette, with the diversity of human skin tones telling the story of how humanity spread and ultimately conquered the planet. Dr Helen Czerski explores the true masters of colour - which are often the smallest and most elusive - travelling to the mountains of Tennessee to witness the colourful mating display of fireflies, and revealing the marine creatures that can change the hue of their skin in order to hide from the world

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: How Australian cinema celebrates the endurance of outsiders, whether they are newcomers to a strange new land in They're a Weird Mob and Wake in Fright, or locals out of step with the mainstream in Evil Angels, Muriel's Wedding and Priscilla, Queen of the Desert

programme.title: A303: Highway to the Sun

programme.title: Mimi & Co
programme.desc: Mimi is on a mission to debunk stereotypes by showing that you don’t have to be just one thing in life. She celebrates people who inspire her by following their dreams and doing what they love. One of those people is Tesha, an old school friend who runs a salon, loves hair and make-up, but also plays football in the FA Women’s National League and internationally. We also meet Nora, who has albinism and is living her dream as a fashion model. And Mimi hosts her Q&A with SBTV creator Jamal Edwards MBE, who shares his own journey to success, and how he has helped the likes of Ed Sheeran. Mimi’s family and friends make a surprise appearance for the end of the series and offer words of wisdom for anyone wanting to chase their own dreams.

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Jahawi is an underwater camera operator who is passionate about preserving the coastline of Kenya, his home country. The ocean is home to a whole host of marine wildlife, but one species here is particularly under threat: dolphins. This fun and curious mammal is facing a number of issues, as their habitat is being destroyed and the fish they eat are being depleted. There is also significant human activity on this coastline, which is damaging the marine life. However, Lamu has stood the test of time and is a marine sanctuary for them, with a new port being built and things about to change. Working with the Kenyan Marine Mammal Network, Jahawi is hoping to get the area protected as a marine reserve before it’s too late. It doesn’t take long for Jahawi to find the resident bottle-nosed dolphins as they enjoy swimming at the bow of the boat, and he soon gets into the water with the dolphins to get a closer look at these magnificent creatures. here is one species of dolphin he is hoping to

programme.desc: It's the hottest day of the year and Waffle is eating ice cream to cool down. Outside in the sweltering heat, Simon turns on the sprinkler to help everyone cool down, but poor Waffle doesn't like the sprinkler and darts through the hole in the fence to Mrs Hobbs's garden, looking for shade. The Brooklyn-Bells try to coax him back, but he refuses to return. Doug decides to make a mini swimming pool by filling the ball pit with cool water. Waffle loves swimming pools. Maybe that will encourage him back?

programme.desc: Storm Huntley visits a family farm and learns how carrots are harvested. JB Gill heads to a spring farmers' market and sees all the seasonal produce. He also discovers how alpacas provide wool - just as sheep do - and gets to take one for a walk. Storm shares a new spring poem and reveals how flour is made from wheat

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog's newspaper boat blows away and the squirrels race to rescue it

When he runs away from the ride, Bob finds him, only for Bevel to admit he's nervous about going in circles. Everyone joins in to help him overcome his fear and soon he's sitting on his seahorse, Blue Yah, excited about his first turn on a merry-go-round. But now the key that winds it up has broken, so Bitz needs to work out how to get it started using wind power

programme.desc: Woody finds out all about superhero spiders! Ferne meets a tarantula and feeds a wolf spider at the zoo, while Rory and his son Sandy go on a spider safari in the garden and at the park. Plus, we find out how spiders are amazing predators that are great for the environment.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Phinehas and Charlie as they both wait for their newborn baby brother or sister to arrive into the world. Charlie’s mum packs a bag ready to go to hospital with dad and waves goodbye to Charlie and Grandma, whilst Phinehas’ family gets ready to pop a gender reveal balloon to find out if the next arrival will be a boy or a girl. Before the big moment, Charlie wraps up a cuddly elephant toy with his Grandma as a gift for his new brother or sister. After a long wait, it’s finally time for Charlie and Phinehas to meet their new siblings. Both babies travel home to their families and meet their big brothers for the very first time. Once Phinehas meets the new baby, he joins in with dad to register the birth of their new baby. Charlie meets a health visitor, who has come to visit to check on the baby and make sure it is staying healthy and strong.

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: Ideas for activities to explore our senses together. Learn how seeing, touching and smelling things are an important part of your toddler’s development. Join in with Heads, Shoulders, Knees and Toes! Hosted by Giovanna from her own home.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Treasure Champs

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects that are associated with the colour white, from snow to space shuttles, and explore how the colour white is used. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour white.

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie to discover the plants that we use in our everyday lives and how we can help to look after them and our planet. We use plants like bamboo to build with, we make clothes from plants, and we even turn plants into useful materials like rubber for making wellington boots. And wood we use for musical instruments! Along the way, we discover how latex comes from inside a rubber tree, and Maddie goes high into the rainforest canopy to show us how tall a bamboo plant grows. She also makes a bowl from a banana leaf.

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Arthur Sleep is joined by team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt in this slapstick quiz show featuring guests and clips from Gigglebiz. Joining Gail and Keith for today’s GiggleQuiz are Wizard Tripwick and Captain Adorable. Round 1 features clips starring Enrico Paso Doble cleaning windows and Keith Fitt trying his hand at karate, only to be bothered by a pesky fly. At the end of Round 1, Keith is annoyed to see that the fly from the clip has followed him into the studio. Always on hand to save the day, Captain Adorable adorns his Adora-Goggles and attempts to catch the fly in his net. Unfortunately, Captain Adorable mistakes Keith for the fly and chases him around the studio instead! There are more flies in Nana’s Panto Showdown as Nana and her Spiders try to get as many flies as possible stuck in their giant sticky web. At the halfway point, Gail’s team are in the lead with two gigglepoints. Round 3 features a clip starring Gail Force at a campsite. Just as Keith thinks the fly has

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome little ones Oliver, Reau, Minnie, Theo, Francis and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Nigel has a toy umbrella inside his bag. All the parents and babies have different weather-themed toys or objects too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide a baby-appropriate weather-themed toy or object inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, to explore with their baby. Some viewers at home show how they’ve been discovering the weather too. The nursery rhyme I See Sunshine features, as does a story about Baby Bear cloud spotting. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Lawrie the Little Owl likes the quiet life. He has no time for adventures and no longing to explore anywhere outside of the garden. But Lawrie’s world gets turned upside down when Parker the Pheasant mistakenly overhears Lawrie reciting a poem and assumes he’s a famous explorer. Rumour spreads around the garden, and it’s not long before everyone is rushing to get a glimpse of this most famous of owls. It’s not the perfect quiet morning that poor old Lawrie had in mind.

programme.title: Colours

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 10-month-old Teddy and Ada as they discover the world around them and learn to communicate through signs and words. Ada and Teddy are both at home playing with toy phones in the garden. Teddy’s Mum takes him to a BSL class where they play with bubbles to help them learn some signs. Ada goes to a signing class and learns the signs for “more” and “milk”. Teddy then visits a butterfly house and gets to see some reptiles and meerkats! After lunch, Teddy’s siblings show him how to use a shape sorting toy, and Ada visits the park to play a game of find the tennis ball. Finally, Teddy practices the signs he learned with his family. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster has been chosen as the prize-picker at the Harvest Day contest, but when he gets there it turns out there is only one prize and three teams. This means he has to choose a winner between his friends - Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two - the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. Love Monster has no idea how he can pick a winner and runs off and hides, but then he realises Bad Idea Puppy always makes him laugh, so he declares him the winner.

programme.title: Octonauts

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Elder Panda is teaching Love Monster to fly a kite. Love Monster loves to watch it swish around in the air. Elder Panda goes off to get some froyo, which is perfect on Kite Day. Before he goes, he tells Love Monster that soon it will be time for him to go on to his next kiting challenge. Love Monster looks at all the other kites and sees the dragon kite, which is the largest kite of them all. Love Monster’s small kite doesn’t seem that exciting any more. He decides to try the dragon kite for just a minute. At first it seems easy, but then a wind gets it tangled in a tree. The rope breaks, and the kite flies off, causing mayhem in Fluffytown with everyone thinking it is a real dragon invasion. Finally, Elder Panda arrives and manages to bring it down to the ground using an umbrella, and Love Monster comes clean, saying how sorry he is. Elder Panda is disappointed but he does understand, as he too wanted to fly before he could run, but sometimes you have to start small before going big.

programme.title: School of Roars

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘shark’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of sharks! There’s a pointy nose shark and a big fin shark, a speedy shark and a grey and white shark. We meet a swishy-tailed shark, a sand shark, a beady eyed shark and even a nurse shark! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Bing

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, Otis, River and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

Will Sid ever find anyone he can eat with?

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 11-month-old Rudi and Everley as they discover the world around them and see what they do to relieve their teething pain. Rudi and Everley begin their day eating breakfast with their families. Everley and her family are in the garden, looking at each other’s teeth in a mirror and counting how many they have. Rudi then plays with a teething mitten to soothe his teeth, before having fresh fruit. Everley visits her family dentist, where the dentist checks her teeth and talks to mum about the importance of teething. Once home, Everley’s mum gives Everley her very own toothbrush! Mum brushes her own teeth and shows Everley how to brush her teeth. Remember, every child develops differently.
